response:
We are given the 4×4 matrix:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
-5 & -5 & 5 & 1 \\
-3 & -3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We are to find the **determinant** of this matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use cofactor expansion

We can expand along the first row for simplicity:

$$
\det(A) = -5 \cdot \det\left(\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}\right)
- (-5) \cdot \det\left(\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}\right)
+ 5 \cdot \det\left(\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -3 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}\right)
+ 1 \cdot \det\left(\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -3 & -4 \\
-4 & 1 & 1 \\
2 & 2 & 4
\end{bmatrix}\right)
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute each 3×3 determinant

Let’s compute each of these 3×3 determinants.

#### 1. First minor:
$$
M_1 = \det\left(\begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}\right)
$$

$$
= (-3)(1 \cdot 4 - 4 \cdot 4) - (-4)(1 \cdot 4 - 4 \cdot 2) + (-1)(1 \cdot 4 - 1 \cdot 2)
$$

$$
= (-3)(4 - 16) + 4(4 - 8) - 1(4 - 2)
= (-3)(-12) + 4(-4) - 1(2)
= 36 - 16
